original_query: SELECT "t0"."SAL" + "t1"."COMM", SUM("t1"."EXPR0") OVER (PARTITION BY "t1"."JOB" RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING) FROM (SELECT "ENAME", "SAL", "DEPTNO" = 10 AS "EXPR0" FROM "EMP") AS "t0" INNER JOIN (SELECT "ENAME", "JOB", "COMM", "SAL" + "SAL" + 100 AS "EXPR0" FROM "BONUS") AS "t1" ON "t0"."ENAME" = "t1"."ENAME" AND "t0"."EXPR0"
rewritten_query:
SELECT "t0"."SAL" + "t1"."COMM", 
       SUM("t1"."EXPR0") OVER (PARTITION BY "t1"."JOB") 
FROM 
  (SELECT "ENAME", "SAL" FROM "EMP" WHERE "DEPTNO" = 10) AS "t0" 
INNER JOIN 
  (SELECT "ENAME", "JOB", "COMM", 2 * "SAL" + 100 AS "EXPR0" FROM "BONUS") AS "t1" 
  ON "t0"."ENAME" = "t1"."ENAME"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Moved the DEPTNO = 10 filter from the join condition to the WHERE clause in the t0 subquery
Removed redundant join condition on t0.EXPR0 since all rows in t0 already satisfy DEPTNO = 10
Simplified the window function by removing the redundant RANGE BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING clause
